informal: Prove that every compact metric space $K$ has a countable base.
lean:
theorem Rudin_exercise_2_25 {K : Type*} [MetricSpace K] [CompactSpace K] :
  ∃ (B : Set (Set K)), Set.Countable B ∧ IsTopologicalBasis B
reasoning: The informal statement asserts that every compact metric space has a countable base. The Lean signature universally quantifies over types K equipped with a MetricSpace and CompactSpace instance (the standard way to express "every compact metric space"), and asserts the existence of a set B of subsets of K that is both countable (Set.Countable) and a topological basis (IsTopologicalBasis). This matches "K has a countable base" exactly. No extra hypotheses are added and none are dropped; the conclusion is the existence of a countable topological basis, which is precisely what the informal claim demands.
